items.title: Always profile before optimizing
items.avoid: Eyeballing the code and guessing what's slow.
items.why: Most intuition is wrong; you waste days on the wrong problem.
items.example: Even seasoned engineers learn something new from the cumulative-time top-10 every time.

items.title: Sort by both cumulative AND tottime
items.prefer: Cumulative first → spot the slow path; tottime second → spot the actual work.
items.why: Cumulative surfaces routers; tottime surfaces the real CPU work.

items.title: Profile a region, not the whole script (when you can)
items.avoid: Hundreds of lines of output dominated by startup/teardown.
items.prefer: `with cProfile.Profile() as pr: do_the_thing()` or a `@profiled` decorator.

items.title: Use snakeviz for non-trivial profiles
items.prefer: `snakeviz profile.out` icicle chart in the browser.
items.why: Visual proportions make the bottleneck pop instantly.

items.title: Measure before/after — twice
items.prefer: Run baseline → optimize → re-run; report the ratio.
items.why: Without numbers, you don't know if you helped or accidentally regressed.
items.example: 'Before: 6.2s. After: 1.4s. 4.4× speedup.' Now you have evidence.
